channel.title: World Cup Competition 2026
channel.description: Political Science World Cup Prediction Competition

item.title: Netherlands vs Japan
item.dc:creator: Håvard Strand
item.description:
Netherlands vs Japan

Japan deserved a point against the Dutch.

Øyvind and Christian has some space in the lead, with Karin, Hallvard, Linda and Jan Erling in pursuit.

Jan Erling and Christian got the exact right result. Well done!

item.title: Goliath beats David
item.dc:creator: Håvard Strand
item.description:
Germany beats Curacao.

Samuel 17:49. He reached into his bag and took out a stone, which he slung at Goliath. It hit him on the forehead, and Goliath looked back and said: “Nice try”. Except Curacao didn’t score at 17:49, it was 20:45.

Øyvind is still in the lead, but Karin and Christian is right behind. Øyvind actually lost one point, despite having a German victory. His 2-0 estimate yielded 25 points for correct outcome and 26 points deduction for dramatically wrong score. This round’s rockets are Dagfinn (6-0) and Bjørn & Jacob (5-1).

Everyone believed in Germany, but the score differentiated a lot. Only four of us saw Curacao score.

item.title: An administrative mistake
item.dc:creator: Håvard Strand
item.description:
A fairly significant administrative issue

The first point of the agenda is that I have made a horrible mistake. Øyvind Colbjørnsen’s contribution was regrettably omitted. This error has now been corrected, with important implications for our competition. Because not only did Øyvind C correctly guess 1-1 as the result in the Brasil - Morocco game, he has also made more correct predictions than any other participant. Which places him at the very top of the competition.

Haiti vs Scotland

Haiti came close to replicating Qatar’s feat, but in the end Scotland won 1-0. Which is potentially important for our competiton in several ways.

Scotland is unlikely to get points from either Morocco or Brasil, and therefore likely to end up in 3rd with a negative goal difference. This might jeopardize their chances for getting to the knockout stage.

Haiti’s result means that they are most likely to end up with 0 points, and should either Curacao or New Zealand get a point, this will be decisive for the qualitiative question regarding these teams.

Australia vs Turkey

Austalia beat Turkey 2-0 this morning, and thereby more or less securing their progress into the next round. Turkey is not completely without hope, but they might have to beat Paraguay with quite a margin to secure progress.

I haven’t had a chance to fix the coloring issue, so today’s update covers points in all four games played yesterday American time. Øyvind Colbjørnsen enters the standings at the very top, with Karin, Ida, Bjørn and Marina begin significant achievers.

item.title: Brazil vs. Morocco
item.dc:creator: Håvard Strand
item.description:
Brazil vs. Morocco

The first half was amazing, and the best half so far in this cup. The second half was not so good.

Åse is in the lead, followed by Christian, Linda, Jon, Ida and Karin. Elin, Hallvard and Øyvind have been distanced somewhat, and joined by a gang of four. But much can still happen

The colors are a bit off here. My system finds it hard to cope with game 7 being played after game 8. It is a bit late to mend that now.

No less than five players had the correct result from this game, while Karin had 2-2. Jostein and Elin had Morocco as winners, and that could have happened as Allison almost gave away a goal in injury time.

item.title: El Infantinity
item.dc:creator: Håvard Strand
item.description:
Switzerland vs Qatar

A game between Infantino’s original home country and his adopted home country. The man is widely assumed to be “influentialable”, but who will he support today? Or, who will support him? The offside after 15 minutes was interesting, but in the end the game was a draw.

So, what does that with our competiton?

Scoll down to the bottom, and admire Marina’s reenactment of dr. Stockman’s stance. “The strongest man in the world is he who stands most alone”. Linda remain in front of the race, as very little changed after this game.

item.title: US preponderance (updated version)
item.dc:creator: Håvard Strand
item.description:
US beats Paraguay 4-1.

That the 15th ranked team in the world can outplay the 45th ranked team is perhaps not a surprise. But for some of us it was. Particularly the margin.

Linda is alone in the top, with Elin and Hallvard right behind. A strong group of contenders are right behind.

None of us had 4-1 down as the result. Elin and Hallvard had 3-1 as their result, and were rewarded handsomely. This is the dot right below the marker in the plot. Christian had 3-2, which was almost as good. 6 of us had 1-0, resulting in 15 points rather than the full 25.

item.title: Canada vs. Bosnia-Herzegovina: The first draw
item.dc:creator: Håvard Strand
item.description:
Canada vs. Bosnia-Herzegovina - One point each

Both teams got a point, but who should be the happier with that result? Probably Linda and Jan Erling, who are ahead.

item.title: Meet your opponents
item.description:
We are 35 participants in this competition. The table below lists all names and abbreviations, and these might be used occasionally throughout the competition.

We mainly fancy France and Spain to win the tournament. Some of us Brazil and England, and then Øivind and Jan Erling have Argentina as their winner, while Jana goes with Mexico, Jon predict German glory, and Marina favors Portugal.

The network graph shows similarity between us. The numbers indicate how many percent of the total 4600 points a given player gets if the node on the other end happens to be 100% correct. If Jon’s predictions were to become reality, Martin would not even get 2000 points. Maiken and Jonas are joint hubs in this network. That has traditionally not been a bad place to be.

item.title: South Korea beat Czechia
item.dc:creator: Håvard Strand
item.description:
South Korea vs Czechia

Czechia scored first, but the country with the highest per capita beer consumption got overrun in the second half.

Øyvind, Elin and Hallvard have 50 points – perfect score so far. 6 more contenders have correct result, but some deviance when it comes to the score.

But, it is still early.

item.title: And we are off!
item.description:
Thursday 1th June 2026, Mexico vs South Africa

The format will be very similar to previous years. The color scale tells us what has changed since last game. Deep blue is good. Red is bad.

item.title: Announcing the ISV 2026 World Cup Competition
item.dc:creator: Dagfinn Hagen
item.description:
Dear all good colleagues

Aaaahhh, finally! Days are brighter and the nights are endless. Smiles everywhere. The waiting period is over! The time has arrived! Some even claim it’s better than May 17 –better than Christmas - the climax of the year and the highlight of our lives – it’s the gentlemen’s World Cup in Football! We would like everyone at Department of Political Science to enjoy the feast, and therefore, all of you are invited to attend the traditional ISV tipster competition for the World Cup 2026!

According to the Departments traditions, the competition is divided in both quantitative and qualitative forms. In addition, we encourage all participants to take part in participatory observations, preferably with social interaction as fieldwork method.
